text.content: Implement PII scanner as a mandatory gate on all document ingestion before chunking or embedding. This is an APP 11 (Privacy Act 1988) compliance control and a prerequisite for the RAG pipeline (TKT-0170). Scope: (1) Install spaCy with en_core_web_lg model on OC1 (lightweight, local, no data residency risk). Note: evaluate Microsoft Presidio as alternative — Ken to confirm preference (Presidio = heavier but better entity detection for structured data). (2) Build PII scanner script: scripts/pii-scan.sh <document_path> — returns JSON: {pii_present: bool, entities_found: [], risk_level: [low|medium|high]}. (3) Wire into document ingestion path: before any document is chunked or embedded, must pass scanner. (4) Set pii_present flag on knowledge_documents table record. (5) Block embedding when pii_present=TRUE without explicit Compliance Agent (Lex) approval. (6) Wire pii_detected event to agent_events table — triggers Lex review queue. (7) Build deletion capability: scripts/purge-document.sh <document_id> — removes all chunks and embeddings for a document (data subject deletion right, APP 12). Dependencies: TKT-0164 (Postgres for event logging). Precondition for TKT-0170 (RAG pipeline). Owner: Forge (build), Lex (policy approval). DoD: scanner live, pii_present flag on schema, block gate enforced, deletion script working.

(7) Build deletion capability: scripts/purge-document.sh <document_id> — removes all chunks and embeddings for a document (data subject deletion right, APP 12).
